rulings.Case Title: NATIONWIDE LEGAL INC. VS. WILLIAM D. DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 5, Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVELYN POQUEZ VS. SUNCOR HOLDINGS-COPII, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF EVELYN POQUEZ' Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint. Denied. Granting Plaintiff leave to file a Fourth Amended Complaint does not serve the interests of justice. The proposed amendments add nothing to the existing complaint and therefore denial of this motion does not prevent Plaintiff from presenting a meritorious cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT TOWERS WATSON AMERICA LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained, without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant has not pled adequate facts to support his claims for equitable indemnity. "A right of equitable indemnity can arise only if the prospective indemnitor and indemnitee are mutually liable to another person for the same injury." (Fremont Reorganizing Corp. v. Faigin (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1153, 1176 - 1177.) "The doctrine [of equitable indemnity] applies only among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff." (BFGC Architects Planners, Inc. v. Forcum/Mackey Construction, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 848, 852.) Here, Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant are not jointly or severally liable for the same injury. (Compare First Am. Cross-Complaint, 31, 73 - 81, Exhibit A, with Second Am. Complaint, 213 - 218.) Cross-Complainant has not shown how he can amend the allegations to cure these defects. Both Cross-Complainant's and Cross-Defendant's Requests for Judicial Notice are unopposed, and are thus granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 209 POST STREET, L.P. VS. VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff 209 Post Street, L.P.'S Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 15, Plaintiff 209 Post Street, L.P.'S Motion To Compel. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Renne, as amended, as follows: The motion is granted in part. Defendant Joanna Rees is ordered as follows: 1) provide supplemental responses to the following discovery requests by August 17, 2012: Demands for Inspection of Documents Nos. 1 - 28, verifying the status of all responsive documents, and Form Interrogatories Nos. 15.1, 17.1 and 50.1; and 2) to submit verifications for all supplemental responses by August 17, 2012. If any supplemental responses identify documents that have not yet been provided to Plaintiff, Defendant shall produce said documents by August 17, 2012. Furthermore, Defendant Rees will appear for deposition at the office of Hanson Bridgett LLP, located at 425 Market Street, 26th Floor, San Francisco, California at 10 am on August 17, 2012, continuing day to day until completed. Rees shall pay sanctions in the amount of $ 2,000.00. Payment shall be made payable to Hanson Bridgett LLP and delivered to Plaintiff's attorneys of record by August 17, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CLARENDON AMERICA INSURANCE COMPANY A DELAWARE VS. ARROWPOINT CAPITAL CORPORATION A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Admiral Insurance Company raises a question of fact whether Clarendon America Insurance Company was not primarily liable for the claimed loss, which is not the proper subject of a demurrer. (Transcontinental Ins. Co. v. Ins. Co. of the St. of Penn. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 1296, 1307 - 1308.) Further, the doctrine of equitable subrogation "is broad enough to include every instance in which one person, not acting as a mere volunteer or intruder, pays a debt for which another is primarily liable, and which in equity and good conscience should have been discharged by the latter." (Id. at 1305.) The Request for Judicial Notice is unopposed, and thus granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES L. ELLIS II et al VS. NEW PACIFIC HOTEL INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts Sukhdeviji Patel And Sangita Patel And Their Attorney Of Record; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JAMES ELLIS, JAMES COX, ALDOMAR ALCARAZ, CARIDAD GAONZALES, JESSE JOHNSON Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Sukhdeviji Patel And Sangita Patel And Their Attorney Of Record. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is GRANTED. In this case centering on habitability issues in a single room occupancy hotel, both defendants have propounded identical sets of 228 specially prepared interrogatories to each plaintiff, for a total of 1,824 specially prepared interrogatories. Defendants have failed to demonstrate justification for exceeding the 35 interrogatory limitation of CCP 2030.040. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK N.A. VS. ANDREW M. PENNELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 13, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT ANDREW PENNELL's Motion To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Citibank's agent, Pam Cline executed her verification of Citibank's response to requests for admissions twelve days before the date on the responses themselves. The verification states that "[T]he matters stated in the aforementioned responses are true . . . ." The responses are dated June 12, 2012 - twelve days later. It is impossible for Ms. Cline to have read responses dated June 12, 2012 in May. She may have read an earlier draft, whether identical or not, but could not have read the document she purports to verify. The responses are not properly verified. Plaintiff Citibank could have easily cured the defect by having Ms. Cline read the July 12, 2012 responses and then sign a new verification dated on the date she actually reads the July 12, 2012 responses, but chose not to. However, since Plaintiff served a hybrid response consisting of both answers and objections, Defendant is not entitled to an order deeming the matters admitted, but rather is entitled to an order compelling verified responses. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $1027.50 is granted. The Court notes that it appears that Plaintiff's opposition was served one day late, but has nonetheless considered it. The envelope in which the opposition was sent appears to have a Pitney Bowes stamp dated August 31st while the proof of services testifies under penalty of perjury that the declarant deposited the envelope, postage prepaid, the day before. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin discloses that thirteen years ago he represented a separate Citibank company, Citibank F.S.B. in litigation unrelated to collection actions. The parties are encouraged to present any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
